festivals.name: Naadam Festival
festivals.description: Mongolia's greatest festival: horse racing, archery, and wrestling.

festivals.name: Regional Naadam
festivals.description: Smaller regional celebrations across the country with local competitions in a more intimate setting.

destinations.name: Gorkhi-Terelj National Park
destinations.description: Rocky outcrops and ger camps 60 km from Ulaanbaatar, ideal for horse trekking.

destinations.name: Khustai National Park
destinations.description: Home to the reintroduced Przewalski's horse — the world's last truly wild horse species.

destinations.name: Orkhon Valley
destinations.description: A UNESCO World Heritage valley with a stunning waterfall, ancient ruins, and lush nomadic landscapes.

destinations.name: Karakorum
destinations.description: The ancient capital of the Mongol Empire; visit the Erdene Zuu Monastery and Inca-age ruins.

destinations.name: Khövsgöl Lake
destinations.description: One of Asia's largest freshwater lakes, the 'Blue Pearl of Mongolia', ringed by taiga and reindeer herders.

destinations.name: Gun-Galuut Nature Reserve
destinations.description: A pristine reserve combining steppe, wetlands, and mountains — less visited and more authentic than closer parks.

destinations.name: Gobi Desert
destinations.description: Singing sand dunes, dinosaur fossil cliffs, and camel trekking across one of the world's great deserts.

destinations.name: Bayan-Olgii
destinations.description: Kazakh eagle hunter territory in the Altai Mountains, home to the Golden Eagle Festival each October.
